versions.label: BSB
paragraphs: 8 The Lord has sent a message against Jacob,
and it has fallen upon Israel.
9 All the people will know it—
Ephraim and the dwellers of Samaria.
With pride and arrogance of heart
but we will rebuild with finished stone;
but we will replace them with cedars.”
11 The LORD has raised up the foes of Rezin against him
12 Aram from the east and Philistia from the west
have devoured Israel with open mouths.
Despite all this, His anger is not turned away;

context_lines: In **Isaiah 9**, this section should be read within the flow of the chapter and the larger movement of Isaiah.
common_interpretation_lines: A common reading of **Judgment against Israel’s Pride** is that it should be interpreted in light of the chapter's full narrative movement rather than as an isolated proof text.
Where interpreters differ on details, the immediate setting in **Isaiah 9** remains the first control on meaning.
